title: HTTP 500 error due to invalid volume path in Docker
symptom: When trying to run an n8n container in Docker Desktop, a HTTP 500 error occurs with an 'invalid mode' message.
applies_when: Using Windows as the host OS
not_this_if: No error occurs when running other containers
root_cause: Docker on Windows requires Linux-style paths for volume mappings, and using Windows-style paths can lead to errors when the container tries to access the specified directories.
fix_steps: Open your Docker Desktop settings for the n8n container.
Change the volume mapping from 'C:\Users\8\n8n-data' to '/home/node/.n8n'.
Ensure the host path is set to 'C:\Users\8\n8n-data' and the container path is '/home/node/.n8n'.
agent_instruction: Update the volume mapping to use Linux-style paths and restart the container.
